LanguageData.EM.SubTab.FluidsGirlJuicePlaceholder: Girl juice (vaginal fluids) — not implemented yet.
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_Mastitis: Mastitis/clog: master switch enables the mechanic; sliders set base MTB, over-fullness and hygiene risk multipliers, and humanlike/animal multipliers. Does not choose hygiene data source (that is set on the DBH sub-tab).
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_DBH: When Dubs Bad Hygiene is installed, choose whether mastitis/clog hygiene risk uses DBH's Hygiene need (pawn hygiene bar) or vanilla room cleanliness. Checked = DBH; unchecked = room cleanliness.
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_ToleranceOverflow: Whether tolerance affects milk efficiency and the impact exponent; which filth def spawns when pool overflows; whether milking AI prefers fuller targets. Reference line shows pool model baselines.
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_IdentityAndMenu: Controls whether right-clicking a pawn of each type shows "Give prolactin", "Breastfeed/suckle", etc. in the float menu. Check by target identity (mechanoid/colonist/slave/prisoner/animal/misc) to reduce clutter.
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_DefaultByRole: These are the defaults for this role (colonist/slave/prisoner/animal/mechanoid/entity). New or unset pawns use these; you can override per pawn in the Milk main table.
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_Efficiency: Efficiency and milk amount multipliers by lactation severity; consciousness/manipulation/moving gain and cap percent; whether adult female animals are always lactating. Affects only this mod's lactation and milk output.
LanguageData.EM.LactationEfficiencyMovedHint: Lactation efficiency and consciousness/manipulation/moving gain are under Integration → Breast & pool, with capacity, flow and remaining days. Only animal "always lactating" remains here.
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_RJW: Active when rim.job.world (RJW) is enabled. Controls lactation–breast size, sex need, post-nursing satisfaction, fertility, sex description, and post-sex milk pool boost.
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_BreastPool: The first option "Lactating enlarges RJW breasts" controls left/right pool capacity and flow source (on = RJW breast data, off = symmetric dual pool, both sides fill together). Below: capacity coefficient, flow multiplier, remaining days, lactation efficiency and gain. See Docs for multi-dose or larger breasts.
LanguageData.EM.RjwBreastSizeSectionHint: Dual-pool data source (checkbox below; check to enable):
LanguageData.EM.SectionDesc_NutritionIntegration: Food ↔ milk pool: mechanoid nutrition→energy factor; lactation extra hunger; reabsorb on shrink. Same logic as in Core → Breastfeed.
LanguageData.EM.BreastfeedOverviewDesc: Who can feed whom is set below by humanlike / animal / mechanoid. Single feeding duration and mechanoid nutrition→energy here.
LanguageData.EM.LactatingGainDesc: When enabled, prolactin-driven lactation steadies the body: clearer head, surer hands. The percentage sets strength (0–20%).
LanguageData.EM.LactatingEfficiencyMultiplierDesc: How much milk one milking or one nursing/suckling yields (default 1.25). Higher = more milk per squeeze or per feed at the same pool fullness. Does not change how fast the pool fills (that is «Flow multiplier» above). More prolactin / higher Lactating stacks = this multiplier stacks more.
LanguageData.EM.FedByColumnTip: For the pawn being fed: choose which lactating pawns are allowed to feed this one. Empty list = any; non-empty = only listed.
LanguageData.EM.ProducerRestrictionsColumn: Who can use my milk / eat my milk or cum products
LanguageData.EM.ProducerRestrictionsColumnTip: Female (lactating): who can use my milk, who can eat my milk products. Male: who can eat my cum products. Different from "Fed by" which is set on the baby.
LanguageData.EM.MilkTagsTableHeaderDesc: Whether milk/cum product info shows race or producer name. "Show animal names" must be enabled for producer restrictions (who can eat my products) to apply.
LanguageData.EM.MilkFullnessColumnTip: Pool fullness (0–100%). Shows "-" if the pawn has no EM lactation comp.
LanguageData.EM.MilkRemainingDaysColumnTip: Estimated remaining lactation days (from L and baseline duration). Shows "-" if not lactating or permanent.
LanguageData.EM.MilkEquivalentDoseColumnTip: Current L expressed as equivalent prolactin doses (read-only). One dose ≈ 0.5 L.
LanguageData.EM.LactatingColumnTip: Whether the pawn is lactating. Blue = temporary, red = permanent (stack count).
LanguageData.EM.ProducerRestrictionConsumersRequiresT: This restriction only applies when "Show pawn name" is enabled for that product type under Settings → Milk tags. Currently off, so the list is hidden.
LanguageData.EM.ProducerRestrictionsHint: Female: who can use my milk, who can eat my milk products. Male: who can eat my cum products. Set per producer in the Milk table — click "Specify" (…) on the row.
LanguageData.EM.DefaultSucklerRulesSection: Default "who can use my milk" (when list is empty)
LanguageData.EM.DefaultSucklerIncludeChildrenDesc: When checked, children of the producer are allowed by default.
LanguageData.EM.DefaultSucklerIncludeLoverDesc: When checked, lovers are allowed by default.
LanguageData.EM.DefaultSucklerIncludeSpouseDesc: When checked, spouses are allowed by default.
LanguageData.EM.DefaultSucklerExcludeParentsDesc: When checked, the producer's parents are not allowed by default.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsTableHeaderBreastfeedTip: Allow this pawn to breastfeed/suckle directly.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsTableHeaderMilkingTip: Allow this pawn to use a milker on this producer.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsTableHeaderSuckleTip: Allow this pawn to suckle or use a milker on this producer.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsTableHeaderConsumeTip: Allow this pawn to consume this producer's milk/cum products (including cooked food).
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsFilterEmpty: No pawns match the current filter.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsFilterEmptyHint: Switch the tab above (All / Colonists / Prisoners) or wait for pawns of that type to join the map.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsAllowSelf_MilkingTip: Allow self to use a milker or take the self-milking job.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsAllowSelf_ConsumeTip: Allow self to consume your milk/cum products (including cooked food). Off = you cannot eat your own products.
LanguageData.EM.RestrictionsAllowOthers_MilkingTip: Allow other colonists/work types to milk this pawn with a milker or milk job. Off = only self-milking can apply (if allowed below).
LanguageData.EM.DrugRoleHint: To enter lactation: Prolactin or Lucilactin. To boost milk output when already lactating: Galactogogues.
LanguageData.EM.RjwBreastSizeDesc: When on: lactating temporarily adds to RJW breast Severity (visual size), and pool capacity plus milk flow use RJW breast data (Severity × capacity coefficient, fluidMultiplier). When off: no size change and capacity/flow do not use RJW breasts.
LanguageData.EM.RjwBreastPoolCapacityModeTip: Three modes only: Severity×coeff, RJW breast weight (kg)×coeff, or RJW BreastSize.volume (L)×coeff. Weight/Volume modes yield 0 capacity (no pool entries) without valid RJW size. Severity mode uses part severity only. Then × capacity coefficient (capped). Flow is separate (GetFluidMultiplier).
LanguageData.EM.RjwBreastPoolTopologyModeTip: Chest unified: lactating breast Hediffs on Chest (or no part) merge into one virtual pool (BreastLeft key). Virtual L/R: default—Breast/MechBreast leaf parts aggregate into left/right virtual slots. Per leaf: one pool per leaf with a stable path key (no L/R merge). When the set of pool keys changes, total stored milk is re-split across the new keys by their base capacity (then clamped per pool to stretch cap), so mass is conserved where possible. Letdown reflex and inflammation I are remapped the same moment (milk-weighted blend, or simple average if no milk snapshot). Full-pool stasis tick counts for mastitis/letters follow the same key migration (integer-conserving split by capacity).
LanguageData.EM.RjwNippleStageFlowBonusPctDesc: When RJW breast stage label contains “Nipple”, multiply lactation inflow / orgasm milk fluid bonus by (1 + this%/100). 0 = off. Clamped to about ±15%.
LanguageData.EM.RjwBreastCapacityCoefficientDesc: Scales the base from the mode above (default: RJW volume in liters; or Severity or weight). 1 = unchanged scale. Tune together with flow multiplier and lactation efficiency.
LanguageData.EM.RjwBreastCapacityCoefficientDescLong: Per-side capacity = base from selected mode × this coefficient (capped); total = left + right. Default Volume mode maps RJW liter scale into pool units; tune to taste. See Docs.
LanguageData.EM.RjwLactatingSeverityBonusDesc: Added to base RJW breast Severity while lactating (pool 0–1). Default 0.15.
LanguageData.EM.RjwLactatingStretchSeverityBonusDesc: Added when pool is in stretch range (1–1.2). Default 0.05.
LanguageData.EM.DefaultFlowMultiplierForHumanlikeDesc: Global multiplier for milk flow (pool units/day). With capacity and baseline days, determines fill time and remaining days for a single dose. Default 2 ≈ single dose fills in ~1 game day.
LanguageData.EM.BaselineMilkDurationDaysDesc: Drives L decay: remaining days for a single dose (L≈0.5) ≈ this value. Default 5 ≈ 5 days; multi-dose total < doses × this.
LanguageData.EM.BirthInducedMilkDurationDaysDesc: Duration for birth-induced lactation (used for L birth-component decay, separate from drug baseline).
LanguageData.EM.BreastPoolParamsHint: Capacity, fill time and remaining days are determined by the sliders above; see parameter table and breast capacity doc.
LanguageData.EM.BreastPoolParamsInToleranceHint: Drug/birth baseline days are under Integration → Breast & pool, with capacity and flow.
LanguageData.EM.RjwLustFromNursingDesc: After nursing, mother and baby gain sex need satisfaction.
LanguageData.EM.RjwSexNeedLactatingBonus: Lactating boosts sex need over time
LanguageData.EM.RjwSexNeedLactatingBonusDesc: While lactating, small periodic bonus to Need_Sex.
LanguageData.EM.RjwSexSatisfactionAfterNursing: Sex after nursing gives extra satisfaction
LanguageData.EM.RjwSexSatisfactionAfterNursingDesc: Sex shortly after nursing gives additional Need_Sex.
LanguageData.EM.RjwLactationFertilityDesc: Pregnancy chance multiplier when mother is lactating (0-1).
LanguageData.EM.RjwLactatingInSexDescDesc: Lactating pawns may get different RJW sex descriptions.
LanguageData.EM.RjwSexAddsLactationBoostDesc: When enabled, lactating pawns who have sex gain a small milk pool increase (ΔL). Uses tolerance and race multiplier.
LanguageData.EM.RjwSexCountsAsStimulusDesc: When enabled with "Sex adds lactation pool boost" above, sex also updates last-drain time and stimulus buffer, slowing L decay (similar to milking/nursing).
LanguageData.EM.UseDubsBadHygieneForMastitis: Use DBH Hygiene need for mastitis risk
LanguageData.EM.UseDubsBadHygieneForMastitisDesc: When enabled, low Hygiene (from Dubs Bad Hygiene) increases mastitis/clog risk. When disabled, room cleanliness is used instead.
LanguageData.EM.AllowMastitisDesc: When disabled, mastitis will not be triggered by full pool, hygiene or injury.
LanguageData.EM.EnableInflammationModelDesc: When off, per-side inflammation I, drain relief, and mastitis-from-I are skipped. MTB mastitis (full pool, hygiene, injury) still follows Allow mastitis.
LanguageData.EM.InflammationAlphaDesc: Multiplier for stasis term: alpha times (excess above threshold) to the exponent; excess is side fullness P minus stasis threshold (vs stretch cap).
LanguageData.EM.InflammationBetaDesc: Per in-game hour added to I on each side when torso/breast injury is present.
LanguageData.EM.InflammationGammaDesc: Scales hygiene risk into I; hygiene coupling rises as stasis (excess fullness) increases.
LanguageData.EM.InflammationRhoDesc: Per in-game hour fractional decay of I (higher = faster recovery without draining).
LanguageData.EM.InflammationCritDesc: When max I across sides reaches this, mastitis can trigger if Allow mastitis is on. Milk quality can raise effective crit.
LanguageData.EM.InflammationStasisFullnessThresholdDe: Side fullness P (vs stretch) below this adds little stasis-driven I; above it, stasis ramps up.
LanguageData.EM.InflammationStasisExponentDesc: Higher means stasis penalty rises sharply only when very full.
LanguageData.EM.InflammationHygieneBaselineFactorDesc: At low stasis, hygiene term is scaled toward this (0 = hygiene barely affects I unless quite full).
LanguageData.EM.InflammationDrainReliefScaleDesc: Per drain event, I drops about scale times (drained amount / side stretch cap), capped below.
LanguageData.EM.InflammationDrainReliefMaxPerEventDes: Maximum I reduction per side per single milking or drain pass.
LanguageData.EM.MastitisInfectionRiskFactorDesc: Medical alignment: when hygiene is bad AND (pool long full or torso/breast injury), MTB is further divided by this factor (higher mastitis risk, mimics bacterial entry). 1 = no extra; suggest 1.1–1.5.
LanguageData.EM.MastitisMtbDaysMultiplierHumanlikeDes: Multiply base mastitis MTB for humanlike pawns. >1 = less frequent mastitis.
LanguageData.EM.MastitisMtbDaysMultiplierAnimalDesc: Multiply base mastitis MTB for non-humanlike (animals). Used when animal mastitis is enabled.
LanguageData.EM.AllowToleranceAffectMilkDesc: When disabled, prolactin tolerance does not reduce lactation flow or duration (E_tol = 1).
LanguageData.EM.OverflowFilthDefName: Filth def name when pool overflows
LanguageData.EM.BaselineMilkDurationReference: Reference: drug baseline {0} days, birth-induced {1} days (each component decays with its own baseline).
LanguageData.EM.AiPreferHighFullnessTargetsDesc: When enabled, colonists will prefer to milk pawns with higher pool fullness first (reduces overflow risk).
LanguageData.EM.RaceCanAlwaysLactate: Races that can always lactate (defName, comma-separated)
LanguageData.EM.DefaultFlowMultiplierForHumanlikeDesc: Multiplier for milk flow for all pawns (1 = normal). Use with RJW/Race mods for balance.
LanguageData.EM.OverflowResidualFlowFactorDesc: When a side reaches the near-full threshold of stretched capacity, flow pressure is at least this value (0–100%), so milk can still trickle in and overflow filth can build. Turn off by setting to 0%.
LanguageData.EM.OverflowResidualDynamicScaling: Scale residual by lactation L and inflammation I
LanguageData.EM.OverflowResidualDynamicScalingDesc: When on: effective residual = base × L/(L+L_ref) × (1 + boost×I/I_crit), clamped to 1. Low L reduces leak; high inflammation increases it (duct congestion / infection).
LanguageData.EM.OverflowResidualLactationRefLDesc: Higher values require more lactation level L before residual leak reaches full strength.
LanguageData.EM.OverflowResidualInflammationBoostDesc: At inflammation I equal to I_crit, the residual multiplier is (1 + this value). 0 = no extra leak from inflammation.
LanguageData.EM.LactationLevelCapDesc: When > 0, drug intake above this L value no longer increases flow rate; the excess only extends lactation duration. More realistic: body needs time to use the drug. 0 = disabled.
LanguageData.EM.LactationLevelCapDurationMultiplierDe: When L is capped, the overflow is converted to Severity (duration) times this factor. >1 = longer duration to compensate for no flow increase. Default 1.5.
LanguageData.EM.DevModeBreastPoolTopologyDisabled: Enable “RJW breast & pool” in Balance → Breast & pool to see pool entries.
LanguageData.EM.DevModeBreastPoolTopologyNoEntries: No pool entries (no lactatable breast hediffs or body).
LanguageData.EM.DevModeBreastPoolUiHint: UI columns: Virtual L/R → left column aggregates anatomical left & Site BreastLeft; right column aggregates anatomical right. Chest-unified + multiple entries → “right breast” totals can mislead; trust per-entry Key. Per-leaf → one Key per leaf.
LanguageData.EM.DevModeBreastPoolTopologyTruncated: (List truncated in UI; full keys in save/tooltip or topology doc.)
LanguageData.EM.DevModeNoPawnSelected: No pawn selected. Select a pawn in-game to see L, pool L/R, E, hygiene risk, overflow.
LanguageData.EM.DevModeNoGame: Not in play. Open a save to see selected pawn lactation info.
LanguageData.EM.LactationLog: Lactation key-path log: birth, drug inflow, hediff add/remove (DevMode)
LanguageData.EM.LactationPoolTickLog: Log each 60-tick: nutrition deducted, pool added, reabsorbed, sucked (DevMode)
LanguageData.EM.LactationDrugIntakeLog: Drug intake debug: log Δs, inflow ΔL, remaining days change on each dose
LanguageData.EM.FullPoolLetterText: {0} has been at full milk pool for over a day; consider milking to avoid discomfort or overflow.
LanguageData.EM.EnableFullPoolLetter: Send letter when milk pool full too long
LanguageData.EM.EnableFullPoolLetterDesc: When enabled, a reminder letter is sent if a colonist's milk pool stays full for over one day. Cooldown below limits how often the same pawn can trigger another letter.
LanguageData.EM.FullPoolLetterCooldownDaysDesc: Minimum days between two reminder letters for the same pawn. Default 2.
LanguageData.EM.AlertLactatingButFluidZero: Lactating but RJW breast fluid multiplier 0; use Dev Mode, RJW: Edit parts.
LanguageData.EM.PoolSuppressionHint: Long time without draining → lactation suppression ↑ → natural end
LanguageData.EM.LactatingStateFullShrinking: Pool full, shrinking (reabsorbed milk adds to food)
LanguageData.EM.MilkFlowPressureWhenFull: (when full, pressure reduces flow; small inflow continues)
LanguageData.EM.ReabsorbNutritionEnabled: Reabsorb nutrition when pool shrinks (add to food need)
LanguageData.EM.PoolBreastUnifiedLayoutLine: Layout: one pool per breast hediff (no L/R split)
LanguageData.EM.PoolFlowEstrogenNote: (Capacity and flow are affected by mammary development and other factors, including estrogen and related physiology.)
LanguageData.EM_CumBucket_LinkBed_Desc: Link this cum bucket to a bed. When empty, only the bed owner can use it; when occupied, others get "not their cum bucket". Shared bedroom (multiple beds in room) = everyone can use, output is mixed cum.
LanguageData.EM_CumBucket_UnlinkBed_Desc: Unlink this bucket from the bed; anyone can use it afterwards.
LanguageData.EM_CumBucket_SharedRoom: Shared bedroom (everyone can use, mixed cum)
LanguageData.EM_CumBucket_NotYours: {0} cannot use: not their cum bucket (already occupied).
LanguageData.EM_CumBucket_CannotLinkNotOwner: The cum in the bucket does not belong to the bed owner; cannot link.
LanguageData.EM.Milk_SealCumTip: Whether to seal and prevent natural cum leakage. Only for humanlike pawns with vagina.
LanguageData.EM.Milk_AllowDeflateTip: Whether this pawn may take deflate jobs (to bucket / clean / ground). Off = no auto deflate.
LanguageData.EM.DefaultSetting_CanBeFedTip: When this pawn is a baby/child: allow caregivers to auto breastfeed them. Off = they won't be chosen as nursing targets.
LanguageData.EM.AdaptationSlowThetaDesc: Slow-phase growth of structural capacity. Higher value means long-term capacity climbs faster under sustained fullness.
LanguageData.EM.AdaptationSlowOmegaDesc: Slow-phase decay of structural capacity. Higher value means long-term remodeling falls back faster when stimulus is low.
LanguageData.EM.InflowEventSubstepsDesc: When event burst is active, each 60-tick update is split into N substeps for smoother inflow and pressure feedback.
LanguageData.EM.InflowEventBurstDurationTicksDesc: How long event-driven burst mode stays active after stimulation (nursing/milking/orgasm), in ticks.
LanguageData.EM.DuctHopPenaltyPerEdgeDesc: Network distance penalty per edge from outlet. Higher value makes deeper pools harder to pass through.
LanguageData.EM.DuctInflowInflammationResistanceDesc: How strongly inflammation reduces inflow conductance.
LanguageData.EM.DuctDrainInflammationResistanceManual: How strongly inflammation resists manual drain/suck.
LanguageData.EM.DuctDrainInflammationResistanceMachin: How strongly inflammation resists machine pumping.
LanguageData.EM.DuctMachineSuctionBonusDesc: Extra suction multiplier for machine pumping compared with manual extraction.
LanguageData.EM.DuctDrainPressureBaseDesc: Base pressure term used by drain model, independent of current fullness pressure.
LanguageData.EM.DuctDrainPressureScaleDesc: Pressure gain term. Higher value makes high-pressure pools drain faster.
LanguageData.EM.DuctConductanceMinDesc: Lower clamp for conductance/take factor to prevent complete lockup.
LanguageData.EM.DuctConductanceMaxDesc: Upper clamp for conductance/take factor to prevent runaway drain speed.
